text: We describe Stanford CoreNLP, a set of libraries providing an extensible Java annotation pipeline for processing natural language. This work provides an extensive set of annotations while keeping the architecture lightweight. Our primary annotators include word-level tokenization, sentence-level segmentation, pos tagging, named entity recognition, parsing, and coreference. The original goal of this project was to replace a proliferation of ad hoc scripts with a more comprehensive and consistent API for NLP processing. Today, the "minimal baggage" design of CoreNLP — providing high quality, general-purpose components via a simple and easy-to-use API — has enabled rapid development and widespread use of CoreNLP in both research and production environments, while keeping the system small enough to avoid the overhead associated with more comprehensive frameworks such as UIMA or GATE. We present a high-level overview of the system, discuss the core annotators, and describe the set of languages currently supported, in addition to discussing how easy it is to add new annotators. We conclude by highlighting the simplicity, ease of use, and ease of deployment of Stanford CoreNLP as a premier toolkit for text processing.
explanation: Moderate likelihood: the text is polished, domain-specific, and promotional, which can be produced by AI but is also exactly the kind of description a human author would write for CoreNLP documentation.
